历年位次与招生计划.title: Historical Rankings and Enrollment Plans
历年位次与招生计划.text: From 2021 to 2025, the minimum rankings for Physics Category (including former Science Category) were approximately 7501, 6700, 11837, 10569, and 12096 respectively. Rankings moved forward from 2021 to 2022, then significantly shifted backward to 11837 in 2023 (a backward shift of over 5000 places), slightly forward to 10569 in 2024, and again backward to 12096 in 2025, showing an overall fluctuating backward trend. The baseline ranking for 2025 is 12096. The enrollment plan for this university in Anhui Province for Physics Category: 36 students each year from 2021 to 2023, reduced to 31 in 2024, increased to 39 in 2025, and remains at 39 for 2026, unchanged from 2025. A stable plan implies zero elasticity in supply-demand balance, with competition determined by other factors.

学校层次与声誉.title: University Level and Reputation
学校层次与声誉.text: China University of Political Science and Law is a Project 211 and Double First-Class university. In the 2026 Soft Science China University Rankings, it ranks 56th, with stable rankings in the 50–56 range in recent years, making it a top-tier political science and law institution in China. The reputation evaluation for 2026 is slightly negative, involving early campus safety incidents, but these events are relatively old and have limited impact on current application psychology. On Douyin, it ranks 6th in popularity (comprehensive popularity approximately 9.75 million), evaluated as high popularity, with extremely high attention on short video platforms. Famous figures like Professor Luo Xiang frequently promote the university, resulting in significant positive exposure. Law is an A+ discipline (the highest national level) and the university's absolute flagship. Among Physics Category majors, Law (including experimental classes) accounts for a high proportion, and the A+ rating in Law strongly attracts high-scoring science students.

专业热度与地域.title: Major Popularity and Region
专业热度与地域.text: The popularity of the Law major in 2024 is characterized by divergence and wait-and-see, but overall remains relatively hot. The total number of Gaokao examinees in Anhui Province in 2026 is approximately 451,000, slightly lower than in 2025 (Physics Category: 320,800). Estimating the Physics Category proportion at about 69.4%, the number of Physics Category examinees in 2026 is approximately 313,000, indicating a slight easing of competition.

预测结论.title: Prediction Conclusion
预测结论.text: Considering the 2025 baseline ranking of 12096, the unchanged enrollment plan for 2026, high Douyin popularity (attracting more applicants and pushing rankings forward), a slight decrease in the total number of Physics Category examinees (easing competition), and the recent fluctuating backward trend in rankings, it is predicted that the minimum admission ranking for Physics Category in 2026 will slightly shift backward compared to 2025. The predicted minimum ranking is approximately 12126, with a range of 11626 to 12626.

历年位次与招生计划.title: Historical Rankings and Enrollment Plan
历年位次与招生计划.text: From 2021 to 2025, the minimum rankings for comprehensive subjects in Zhejiang Province were approximately 22534 → 21723 → 21596 → 16712 → 15626. From 2021 to 2023, they stabilized in the range of 21,000 to 22,000. In 2024, there was a significant forward shift to 16712, and in 2025, it further moved to 15626, with a cumulative forward shift of about 5970 in the last two years, indicating a continuous increase in application popularity in Zhejiang. The enrollment plan for comprehensive subjects in Zhejiang Province: 70 in 2021, 63 in 2022, 55 in 2023, 53 in 2024, and 65 in 2025. For 2026 (according to the enrollment plan document), the plan is 50, a decrease of 15 from 2025, a reduction of about 23.1%, significantly tightening enrollment quotas, theoretically easing competition slightly.

学校层次与声誉.title: School Level and Reputation
学校层次与声誉.text: Harbin Engineering University is a 211 Project and Double First-Class construction university. Its Soft Science Chinese University Ranking has steadily risen from 50th in 2021 to 43rd in 2026, indicating continuous improvement in overall strength and recognition. The school ranks 27th in Douyin's school popularity ranking (among 106 universities), rated as 'relatively hot'. Education bloggers like Zhang Xuefeng have repeatedly recommended its top majors such as underwater acoustic engineering and nuclear engineering. In 2026 reputation data, no major negative incidents were found, with a stable and positive reputation. In the fifth round of discipline evaluation, Control Science and Engineering was rated A, the school's top discipline. Naval Architecture and Ocean Engineering, Underwater Acoustic Engineering, and Nuclear Engineering and Nuclear Technology are traditional advantageous directions, highly matching the enrollment majors.

专业热度与地域.title: Major Popularity and Region
专业热度与地域.text: Engineering majors such as Computer Science, Electronic Information/Communication, and Electrical Engineering and Automation continue to be highly recommended, aligning with the school's engineering characteristics. In 2026, the total number of candidates in Zhejiang Province is about 400,000, a significant increase from 291,000 in 2025, intensifying competition. The provincial control line for the general category in 2025 was 490 points, with little fluctuation in recent years. The school is located in Harbin, Heilongjiang, far from Zhejiang, but as one of the 'Seven Sons of National Defense' and a 211/Double First-Class university, it still has strong appeal to Zhejiang candidates pursuing engineering advantages. The school's enrollment proportion in Zhejiang is about 1.5%–2%, which is normal for out-of-province enrollment.

预测结论.title: Prediction Conclusion
预测结论.text: Considering multiple factors such as the continuous forward shift of historical rankings in the last two years, a significant reduction in the 2026 enrollment plan (-23.1%), the school's rising ranking, positive reputation, and a substantial increase in the number of candidates in Zhejiang Province, the enrollment reduction provides momentum for a forward shift in rankings, but the surge in candidate numbers partially offsets this effect. Based on the 2025 baseline of 15626, the estimated minimum ranking for 2026 is about 13663, with a range of 13163 to 14163.
